Please read this important notice about your recent online order

We have received an online order placed under Order # *4118158* for medication totaling approximately *$400.00*. The item(s) you ordered are classified as prescription-only and cannot be released without a valid, current prescription on file.

*Important:* This medication requires a valid prescription under state and federal law. Dispensing or possessing this medication without a lawful prescription is prohibited and may constitute a criminal offense.
To complete your order and schedule pickup, please provide one of the following as soon as possible:
* A copy of a current prescription faxed or uploaded to our secure portal
* Your prescriber’s name and phone number so we may verify the prescription directly
If we do not receive a valid prescription within *7 business days* , the order will be cancelled and any authorized hold will be released in accordance with our policies.
If you believe you received this message in error, or you wish to dispute or cancel the order, please contact our pharmacy support team immediately using the official phone numbers listed below.
Thank you for your attention to this matter and for helping us maintain safe, lawful dispensing practices.

TKO NOTICE: Possible Registration Suspension - Unauthorized Account Use !,"Dear eBay member Dear eBay member , Due to recent activity, including possible unauthorized listings, we may temporarily suspend activity on your account . If you believe that this action may have been taken in error, or, if you feel that your account may have been tampered with, please Sign In to your account and check for unauthorized listings. If there are no problems with your account please ignore this message and do not reply ! In the event that you have problems and you are unable to contact eBay through your account , respond directly to this message to request assistance. We will contact you by email after we have received your response. Please allow at least 72 hours for an email reply. Emailing us prior to receiving our reply will result in an additional delay. In order to handle your concern as quickly and efficiently as possible, we encourage you to contact us through Live Help if you are able to do so. If you received this notice and you are not the authorized account holder, please be aware that it is in violation of eBay policy to represent oneself as another eBay user. Such action may also be in violation of local, national, and/or international law. eBay is committed to assist law enforcement with any inquiries related to attempts to misappropriate personal information with the intent to commit fraud or theft. Information will be provided at the request of law enforcement agencies to ensure that perpetrators are prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law. Regards, Safeharbor Department eBay Inc.",1

column36: > Dear Ledger User,
column41: > We are thrilled to announce our biggest update to Ledger Live yet! This release is packed with powerful new features designed to give you more control over your digital assets, dive deeper into DeFi, and enhance your security in ways that matter.
column46: > Here’s a look at what’s new:
column49:
>
  • Integrated Cross-Chain Swaps: Seamlessly swap assets across different blockchains directly in Ledger Live, powered by our trusted partners.
  • Advanced Staking Dashboard: Manage all your staking positions from one place. We've added support for new assets and provide detailed reward analytics.
  • Comprehensive NFT Gallery: Your NFT collection now has a beautiful new home. View, manage, and see estimated values for your collectibles on Ethereum, Polygon, and more.
column52:
>
  • Transaction Simulation: Before you sign, see a clear "
column54:
simulation of the transaction's outcome. Avoid nasty surprises and malicious smart contracts.
  • Secure Address Whitelisting: Add trusted addresses to your device for an extra layer of protection, ensuring you only send funds to pre-approved contacts.
  • Upgraded Firmware Resilience: The new device firmware strengthens the secure element’s architecture, providing an even more robust foundation for your private keys.

Electrum Release New Features for Desktop & Browser,"Hello users,

We’re pleased to announce that an updated version of the Electrum wallet is available. This update brings enhanced functionality, broader platform support, and key security improvements. Read on for details and follow the instructions at the end to download and install the update.
* Native installers for macOS (10.14 and later)
* Linux packages for Ubuntu, Fedora, Debian, and other distributions
* Seamless integration with Chrome, Edge, Brave, Vivaldi, and other Chromium browsers
* Using your Electrum wallet without leaving your browser
* Reduced memory usage when handling large address sets
* Optimized network requests for smoother SPV syncing
* Improved SSL certificate validation for Electrum servers
* Optional two-factor authentication plugin (via YubiKey or TOTP)
* Redesigned transaction history layout with filter and search options
* Clearer fee-selection slider with real-time fee estimates
* Dark-mode toggle, synced with your OS setting
* Coin-control plugin with address tagging and label import/export

Keeping your wallet software up to date ensures you benefit from the latest security hardenings and network-protocol compatibility. This version also lays the groundwork for upcoming features (multi-purpose hardware signing, advanced coin-join support, and more).
1. Visit the official Electrum download page:
2. Choose the installer or package that matches your operating system or browser:
3. Download the file and verify its PGP signature if desired (instructions available on the download page).
4. Run the installer or load the extension, then restart Electrum (or your browser) to complete the update.
5. Logon to Electrum and confirm your wallet loads as expected. No change to your seed phrase is required.
If you encounter any issues during the update process or have questions about the latest features, refer to our official documentation or reach out via our support channel.
Thank you for using Electrum. Keeping your wallet current helps us maintain a secure and reliable network for everyone.

What food blogging taught me about my Asian heritage and identity,"Plus, meet some of NPR's AAPI employees. View this email online by Suzanne Nuyen May 20, 2023 Welcome to NPR’s Up First newsletter. We’ll deliver the news you need to start your day every weekday, along with deep dives on complicated issues, entertainment guides, life advice and more. On Sundays, we’ll make sure you didn’t miss anything important with a showcase of the NPR Network’s best journalism and storytelling, mixed with some of our favorite podcast recommendations and weekend fun. Thanks for reading. Good morning. This is a special Asian American and Pacific Islander Heritage Month edition of the Up First newsletter. Meet some of NPR's AAPI employees and read about how we've covered the community so far this month. I'm really into: food bloggingI love to eat, but feeding others is my love language. When I graduated from college and moved to Washington, D.C., in 2017, I left all of my friends behind. Plying new acquaintances with lavish, home-cooked meals was my best attempt at making new friends and curing my loneliness. I don't want to brag, but my food is pretty good. Soon enough, everyone was asking me for the recipes, which is how Bun Bo Bae, my Vietnamese cooking blog, came to be in 2019. Suzanne Nuyen/Bun Bo BaeAt first, I wanted to preserve my family's recipes. My parents passed down their knowledge orally, and I didn't want all of the secret knowledge behind dishes like my mom's bún bò huế (a spicy beef and pork noodle soup that inspired the blog's name) or my dad's thịt băm sốt cà chua (a versatile, pork-based tomato sauce) to disappear to history. I wanted to write down every piece of advice I was given in detail, to make sure people could keep creating these dishes as authentically as possible. These days, I do experiment more. I'm not as strict with myself about preserving and recreating Vietnamese dishes exactly as I remember eating them. I use seasonal ingredients and substitute items when I can't find them. Some recipes, like my bò lúc lắc, or shaking beef, feel entirely my own. As my food blog and follower count grew, so did my confidence in the kitchen. Writing for Bun Bo Bae has taught me that celebrating my Vietnamese American culture doesn't have to be about perfectly re-creating my parents' traditions. It's about mixing what I've been taught with my own experiences, and cooking up something entirely new. — Suzanne Nuyen, NPR editorThis essay was written for NPR's special series, I'm Really Into. You can read the entire piece here. Newsletter continues after sponsor message AAPI Faces of NPR NPRThis year, NPR is celebrating AAPI Heritage Month with the theme ""Telling Our Stories."" Asian Americans are the fastest-growing demographic in the nation, but they remain underrepresented in media, politics, workforce management and school curriculums. Meet a few of NPR's AAPI employees and read about how they're telling their stories: — Gary Duong, senior marketing managerI handle audience marketing for NPR's shows and podcasts. I love that we're a mission-driven organization and that people are proud to be here. Writing isn't in my job description, but I'm proud that I have two published articles on NPR.— Janet Woojeong Lee, education desk producerI manage NPR's Student Podcast Challenge, which is an initiative to get more young, diverse voices on air. I feel most grateful for the friends and mentors I've met here, and through them, I'm constantly learning how to be a better advocate for my community. At NPR, I'm hoping to push for more accurate data and an understanding of our AAPI audience that considers our range and intersectionality.— Linah Mohammad, All Things Considered producerMaking radio that millions of people depend on to get their daily news is a lot of responsibility, but I also find it exhilarating. In my eyes, my mom is my target audience. I ask myself at work, ""If Mama listened to this, would she be able to understand?"" I'm proud of the stories I work on, but the think I'm most proud of achieving at NPR is creating the Southwest Asians and North Africans Employee Resource Group. Read more about the AAPI Faces at NPR here. Picture Show Akash PamarthyTelugu Americans are a huge part of the Indian population living abroad. Between 2010 and 2017, the number of native Telugu speakers in the U.S. rose 86%, making it the largest uptick in a foreign-language group. Through the lenses of family, keepsakes, festivals and food, photographer Akash Pamarthy explores how the Telugu community preserves their culture through the next generation. Stories you may have missed Kori Suzuki for NPRFor decades, Eastwind Books was an anchor for the Bay Area's Asian American community. The husband and wife duo behind it have decided to close the shop, but they say it's like another beginning. 🎧 Listen to community members reflect on the bookstore's history and how it embraced the power of ethnic solidarity. Sesame Street debuted its first Filipino Muppet this month. TJ joins Ji-Young, the show's first Asian American character, who was introduced in 2021. The Pew Research Center released data from a first-of-its-kind poll of 7,000 Asian American adults that sheds light on how Asians — both immigrants and those born in the U.S. — see themselves and others. Ranee Ramaswamy believes her oldest daughter was born to dance. She founded the Ragamala Dance company 30 years ago. Since then, it has become famous among American fans of India's oldest classical dance form, bharatanatyam. Is this the next big discourse book? R.F. Kuang's Yellowface is all critics can talk about this month. It's about a white author who passes off her Asian friend's work as her own after the friend dies. 🎧 Pick up the book and listen to Kuang talk about cultural appropriation on All Things Considered Listen to your local NPR station. Visit NPR.org to hear live radio from KUNC (edit station). This newsletter was edited by Carol Ritchie. Enjoying this newsletter? Forward to a friend! It’s not too late to plan for the long weekend ☀️,"Plus, a question for you View this email online Kaz Fantone/NPRHannah Sampson has two rules for every trip she goes on: She always visits a bookstore (ideally one that sells used books), and she always tries some local ice cream. Beyond books and ice cream, Sampson, a staff writer at The Washington Post who covers travel news for By The Way, has tips on making the most of a long weekend trip. If getting out of town this weekend isn't in the cards for you, here are a couple of other ideas to consider: 🌻 Plan a staycation: Sampson recommends planning a ""tourist"" adventure even if you aren’t leaving home this weekend. Write down a list of activities or places nearby you've wanted to explore but haven’t had the time to check out. Or, go on your usual walk but try and see it with a new lens. Really paying attention to a familiar place, like noticing flowers at a park or learning about local history, can help you play tourist without leaving town. 🏠 Tackle your home: Clean that junk drawer or reorganize your kitchen cabinets. If you're too tired to clean, use this tidying method that’s quick and won't wear you out. If you do feel ready for a deeper clean, remember to go from top to bottom so you're not dusting onto freshly mopped floors. 🎨 Start a creative habit: Creativity is good for your health, but focus more on the process than the final product. Just mess around and see what creative activity engages you. Maybe it's something you did as a kid, like making bracelets or coloring. Some more ideas (not an exhaustive list): sewing, cooking, writing, singing or baking. But don't feel like you have to stick to one thing – the art therapists from our episode on a creative habit told Life Kit you still get the same benefits of reducing stress and anxiety no matter the activity. 🫶 Spend quality time with loved ones: Call a friend who lives far away, try this playtime ritual with a little one, or express your gratitude to someone you've been thinking about. Maybe this weekend is a time to remember lost loved ones – to grieve and celebrate those who aren't with us. However you spend it, enjoy your weekend (whether it’s long or not). -Clare Marie Schneider, Life Kit producer and editor We want to hear from you!What are your tips for enjoying a long weekend? How do you make the most of an extra day off at home or a short trip away? Email us with your full name by Monday, May 29. We may feature your response in an upcoming newsletter or on our website. Newsletter continues after sponsor message Your tips on making friends Last week, we asked readers for tips on making friends in a new place. Here are two of your ideas: (responses have been edited for length and clarity) Try volunteering: ""Find a local group that is doing work you admire and volunteer! I volunteered for a community cleanup day in 2019, and now I'm a co-director of the group
column2: I've met loads of people in the community through our organization's work."" – Lindsay Koehler Create a welcoming space: ""I have moved many times over my lifetime. This last time was to an apartment. To make it clear that I was interested in meeting people, I sat on my patio and waved to people as they walked by. The interested ones would come over to my patio to talk. I decorated my patio with flowers (real and artificial) and added a couple of weird things. At holiday times, I decorated accordingly. Some people would come to my patio to see what I was doing or ask questions about my decorations. I met a best friend because I had a hummingbird feeder."" – Barbara Doebele Access Sponsor-Free EpisodesWant to support our show? Chic and Scrappy: Wooden Couch Sleeve,"Chic and Scrappy: Wooden Couch Sleeve A step-by-step scrap wood project easy enough for the kids to do with you ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ READ THE POSTHave you ever had a place for a chair but a side table just doesn't seem to fit? If you've got some scrap wood and a miter saw, this project can be done in 10 minutes — save the drying time of the stain and glue. This is an great beginner project, so if you're hoping to face your fear of power tools, jump right in! The cuts are super simple and you'll have a solid place to write in your planner, rest your coffee, hide the remote… oh wait, that's just me.P.S. This would be a great project for kids who like hands-on learning too (you do the power tool part, while they do the gluing and staining). 4671 Flint Hill RdAustell, GA 30096, USA We get it—inboxes get out of control, and sometimes you just need a break.
